steps.title: Identify the logical goal
steps.content: The passage states that coins with silver content below 80% were considered unsuitable for trade, causing a crisis of confidence. Abd'astart I's decision must logically increase the silver percentage enough to address that crisis. The 367 BCE coins had 74.2% silver, so the useful change must move the silver percentage upward toward or above the 80% threshold.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘keep the amount of silver in Sidonian coins consistent with that in coins minted in 367 BCE but decrease their weight.’: Holding the absolute silver amount constant while reducing total weight raises the silver percentage above 74.2%; if the weight is reduced enough, this could bring the percentage back above the 80% threshold and restore trade confidence. ✓
  • Choice B — ‘proclaim that the percentage of silver in coins suitable for trade would be raised to a threshold higher than 80%.’: A proclamation about a future standard does not actually fix current coins' silver content or restore confidence in existing currency. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘fund the mining of some copper deposits that were not available to Ba'alsillem II.’: Adding more copper would worsen debasement and further reduce the silver percentage, the opposite of what is needed. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘begin minting heavier coins with a proportion of silver to copper similar to that in coins minted in 367 BCE.’: Maintaining the same 74.2% silver proportion in heavier coins keeps silver content below 80%, failing to resolve the crisis. ✗
That confirms A is correct.
